title: aquilegia vulgaris var. bernardii
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris var. bernardii, also known as the bear's ear or bear's foot, is a variety of the common columbine plant, characterized by its delicate, bell-shaped flowers in shades of purple and white. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with properties that include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of aquilegia vulgaris var. bernardii include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and soothe sore throats, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Aquilegia vulgaris var. bernardii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and has been used in folk medicine to treat a range of health issues, from colds and fever to skin conditions and digestive problems.
